basics.What Git is: Distributed version control system. Every clone is a full backup. Tracks content (files) not filenames. Snapshots (commits) form a DAG of project history.
basics.Repository: Git database in .git/ directory. Objects: commits, trees (directories), blobs (files), tags. References: branches, HEAD, tags. Created with git init or git clone.
basics.Working Directory: Files you edit. Three states: modified (changed but not staged), staged (added to index, ready to commit), committed (safely stored in repo).
basics.Commit: Snapshot of entire project at a point in time. Has hash (SHA-1), parent(s), author, message, tree. Immutable once created. Commit with: git commit -m 'message'.
basics.Branch: Lightweight movable pointer to a commit. Default branch: main (or master). Create: git branch name. Switch: git checkout name or git switch name. Merge combines branches.
basics.Remote: URL-linked copy of repository. origin is default remote name. git push sends commits, git pull fetches+merges, git fetch only downloads. HTTPS or SSH protocols.
basics.Staging Area (Index): Intermediate area between working directory and repo. git add stages changes. git status shows what's staged/unstaged. Allows partial commits with git add -p.

commands.git init: Create new empty repository in current directory. Creates .git/ subdirectory. Use for new projects, then add and commit.
commands.git clone: Copy remote repository: git clone URL. Creates directory named after repo. Sets up remote 'origin'. Common flags: --depth 1 (shallow, faster).
commands.git add: Stage changes for commit. git add file (specific), git add . (all), git add -p (interactive hunk selection). Staging allows selective commits.
commands.git commit: Create commit from staged changes. git commit -m 'message' (inline), git commit (opens editor). -a stages all tracked files first. --amend fixes last commit.
commands.git status: Show working tree state: staged, unstaged, untracked files. -s (short format). Most-used command. Shows branch, ahead/behind remote.
commands.git log: Show commit history. git log --oneline (compact), --graph (branch visualization), --all (all branches), -p (show diffs). --author='name' filters by author.
commands.git diff: Show changes. git diff (unstaged), --staged (staged), HEAD~1..HEAD (between commits). Stat option shows summary. --word-diff for word-level changes.
commands.git push: Upload commits to remote. git push origin main. -u sets upstream (for future git push/pull without args). --force (overwrite, DANGEROUS).
commands.git pull: Fetch + merge from remote. git pull = git fetch + git merge. --rebase uses rebase instead of merge for cleaner history.
commands.git fetch: Download remote changes without merging. Updates remote-tracking branches (origin/main). Safe: doesn't affect working directory.
commands.git merge: Combine branches. Creates merge commit with two parents. Fast-forward if linear history. Conflict markers show when same lines changed. Resolve manually.
commands.git rebase: Reapply commits on top of another branch. Produces linear history (vs merge's branching). Never rebase shared branches (rewrites history). Interactive: git rebase -i HEAD~3.
commands.git reset: Move HEAD and optionally branch. --soft (keep changes staged), --mixed (keep in working dir, unstage), --hard (discard all, DANGEROUS).
commands.git revert: Create new commit that undoes specific commit. Safe for shared history (no rewriting). Opposite of reset: both move forward in time.
commands.git stash: Temporarily save uncommitted changes. git stash push -m 'message', git stash pop (apply+drop), git stash list, git stash drop. Useful for switching branches.
commands.git branch: Manage branches. git branch (list), git branch name (create), git branch -d name (delete merged), git branch -D name (force delete).
commands.git checkout: Switch branches or restore files. Modern: git switch (branch only) and git restore (files only). git checkout -b creates + switches.
commands.git tag: Mark specific commit (releases). git tag v1.0 (lightweight), git tag -a v1.0 -m 'release' (annotated). Pushed separately: git push --tags.
commands.git blame: Show who last modified each line: git blame file. Annotates with commit hash, author, date. Useful for finding why a line was changed.
commands.git bisect: Binary search for bug-introducing commit. git bisect start → good/bad → automatically checks out midpoint. Finds the exact commit that broke something.
commands.git cherry-pick: Apply specific commit(s) to current branch: git cherry-pick HASH. Creates new commit with same changes. Useful for hotfixes across branches.
commands.git clean: Remove untracked files. git clean -n (dry run), git clean -f (force), -d (directories). Removes build artifacts and temp files.
commands.git submodule: Include other repos inside yours. git submodule add URL path. Initializes with --init. Updates with --remote. Pins specific commit in parent.

workflows.Feature Branch: Create branch per feature: git checkout -b feature-name. Commit changes. Push branch. Create PR/merge request. Merge to main when done. Delete feature branch.
workflows.Git Flow: main (stable) + develop (integration) + feature/* + release/* + hotfix/*. Popular but complex. Suitable for scheduled releases. Not ideal for continuous delivery.
workflows.GitHub Flow: Anything in main is deployable. Create branch, commit, push, open PR, review, deploy, merge. Simpler than Git Flow. Works for continuous deployment.
workflows.Forking Workflow: Fork repo on GitHub, clone fork, add upstream remote, make changes in fork, submit PR from fork to upstream. Standard for open-source contributions.
workflows.Cherry-Pick Workflow: Hotfix on main branch, cherry-pick to release branches. Avoids merging main into release. Useful for patch releases.
workflows.Commit Message Convention: Conventional commits: type(scope): description. Types: feat, fix, docs, refactor, test, chore. Examples: 'feat(api): add user endpoint', 'fix(db): handle null constraint'.

troubleshooting.Merge conflict: <<<<<<< HEAD marks your changes, ======= separates, >>>>>>> branch has theirs. Edit to resolve, remove markers, git add and commit. Use git mergetool for GUI.
troubleshooting.Accidental commit to wrong branch: git log to find hash, git checkout correct-branch, git cherry-pick HASH, git checkout old-branch, git reset HEAD~1 --hard.
troubleshooting.Committed but forgot something: git commit --amend to add staged changes to last commit. Don't amend if already pushed (rewrites history).
troubleshooting.Need to undo pushed commit: Use git revert HASH (creates new undo commit, safe). Or git reset then git push --force (DANGEROUS, only on private branches).
troubleshooting.Detached HEAD: HEAD points to commit instead of branch. Create branch to save work: git switch -c new-branch. Or checkout existing branch.
troubleshooting.This is not a git repository: Run git init or git clone first. Check you're in the right directory. Check parent directories don't have .git that shadows.
troubleshooting.Untracked files showing: Normal for new files. Add to .gitignore if they should never be committed (build artifacts, IDE files, secrets). git status shows untracked files separately.
troubleshooting.Can't push (non-fast-forward): Remote has commits you don't have. Run git pull first (or git fetch + git rebase). Then try push again.
troubleshooting.Authentication failed: Check credentials. For HTTPS: use personal access token (not password). For SSH: check ssh-key is loaded and added to GitHub/GitLab.
troubleshooting.Large file committed: Remove with git filter-branch or BFG Repo-Cleaner. Rewrites history (force push needed). Better: use Git LFS for large files moving forward.

github.Pull Request (PR): Proposal to merge changes from one branch to another. Review process: comments, approvals, CI checks, requested changes. Merge options: merge commit, squash, rebase.
github.Issues: Bug reports, feature requests, tasks. Labels for categorization, milestones for planning, assignees for ownership. Can be referenced in commits: 'Fixes #42'.
github.Actions: CI/CD automation. YAML workflows in .github/workflows/. Triggers: push, PR, schedule, manual. Runners execute jobs (GitHub-hosted or self-hosted).
github.GitHub Pages: Free static site hosting from repo. Enable in Settings → Pages. Jekyll support built-in. Custom domain supported. Great for documentation sites.
github.Code Review: Review PRs before merging. Comments on specific lines. Request changes vs approve. Best practices: small PRs, descriptive titles, link to issues.
github.Protected Branches: Require PR reviews before merging. Require status checks (CI passing). Require up-to-date branch. Prevent force push. Admin approval for bypass.
github.README: Repository front page. Should explain: what project does, how to install, how to use, how to contribute. Markdown format. Badges for CI, coverage, version.
